title: 控方证人
summary:
After observing that Madgeleine and Charlotte do not look alike, Mortimer Cleveland confronts Mr. Dinsmoor, deducing that one of the girls is adopted. Mr. Dinsmoor admits that Charlotte is an orphan they adopted, but claims he recently discovered through a lawyer and a newspaper advertisement that Madgeleine is actually the biological daughter of a wealthy Jewish man who intended to leave her a large inheritance. While Cleveland finds the story plausible, he remains suspicious. As Cleveland prepares to depart, he notices Mrs. Dinsmoor acting fearfully while holding a miniature portrait of Charlotte from twenty-five years ago.

Before Cleveland can leave, Madgeleine intercepts him and reveals that she wrote the "SOS" message on a table the previous night. She expresses a deep sense of dread regarding her family, noting that her parents and Charlotte have all changed for the worse, while her brother Johnny remains unaffected because he did not drink the tea. Cleveland realizes the family may be being poisoned, connecting the behavior to a news report about arsenic contamination.

Cleveland returns to the house that evening during dinner. He intercepts the tea service and uses test tubes to demonstrate his theory, accusing Mr. Dinsmoor of intentionally placing arsenic in the bottom of Charlotte's teacup. Cleveland explains that the motive is to make Charlotte's death appear to be an accident caused by contaminated food or tea, thereby ensuring the biological daughter, Madgeleine, inherits the fortune. He deduces that the plan was to eliminate Charlotte to prevent anyone from discovering the truth about Madgeleine's lineage. Madgeleine pulls Cleveland aside, and he tells her he will keep the evidence to protect Charlotte.
